prompt:
Our product-catalog mirror re-downloads the whole upstream dataset every night, and last week the vendor shipped the delta feed we asked for — so now I need the client, package syncdelta, in this module. The feed is at-least-once: after any hiccup it happily redelivers changes you already applied, so the client has to be built for replays from day one. syncdelta_test.go is the acceptance suite (all local httptest with scripted pages); `go test -race -timeout 30s ./...` green means done; the test file is read-only.

The wire protocol: GET {base}/changes returns {"changes": [...], "next_since": "<cursor>", "has_more": bool}. Pass the checkpoint as ?since=<cursor>; when we have no checkpoint yet, the since parameter must be ABSENT (the vendor treats an empty since as a real cursor and 500s, found that out the fun way). Each change is {"op": "upsert"|"delete", "id": ..., "rev": <int>, "data": {string: string}} — data only on upserts.

What I want, shape-wise:
- Record{ID string; Rev int; Data map[string]string} and Change (op/id/rev/data with matching JSON tags).
- NewTable() *Table — the in-memory mirror. Apply(Change) (bool, error) applies one change and says whether it changed anything; Get(id) (Record, bool) and Len() see live records only; IDs() gives sorted live ids. Unknown ops are an error naming the op.
- Rev rules, and these are the whole ballgame for replay safety: an upsert applies only when its rev is strictly newer than whatever we know for that id — the live record's rev OR its tombstone's rev; applying replaces Data wholesale and clears any tombstone. A delete applies when its rev is at least the live rev (equal rev: delete wins) and strictly newer than any existing tombstone; applying removes the record and remembers the tombstone at that rev, and a delete for an id we've never seen still records its tombstone. Skipped changes return false. This is exactly what makes a redelivered page a pile of harmless skips instead of resurrected deletes.
- CursorStore interface {Load() (cursor string, ok bool, err error); Save(cursor string) error} — checkpoints are injectable; ok=false means no checkpoint yet.
- NewClient(hc *http.Client, baseURL string, store CursorStore) *Client with Sync(ctx, *Table) (Stats, error). Sync loops: fetch page, apply every change in order, THEN Save(next_since) — apply-before-checkpoint, so a lost checkpoint means redelivery, never loss — and continues while has_more. Stats{Pages, Upserts, Deletes, Skipped}: Upserts/Deletes count applied changes, Skipped counts the replays, Pages counts pages that fully applied AND checkpointed.
- Failures: non-200 page (status code in the message), undecodable body, a bad op mid-page, or a store error — return the stats so far plus an error; store errors must wrap the original so errors.Is finds it. NO retry logic anywhere: a failed Sync just returns, and the next Sync resumes from the last committed checkpoint. Retry policy belongs to the scheduler that calls us, not this package.
